old_claim.note: Mass Media provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The postwar consumerism scope is driven by advertising-mediated demand creation, so mass advertising is the direct media prerequisite rather than generic mass media.

source_shape.source_locator: The Organization Man section: OpenStax describes postwar purchases of appliances, home goods, second cars, vacations, and college education, and says Madison Avenue began appealing to teenage interests and advertising clothing, cosmetics, and teen magazines.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: OpenStax ties postwar consumer purchasing to Madison Avenue marketing and advertised youth-oriented goods.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports advertising as the demand-creation mechanism; generic mass media is too broad because it also includes news, entertainment, and public communication not directed at consumption.

ontology_before: Consumerism depended directly on broad mass media, blurring advertising-driven consumer demand with all large-audience media systems.
ontology_after: Consumerism depends directly on mass advertising, the narrower existing node that creates and channels demand for mass-produced goods.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: consumerism now depends on advertising_mass_media_early and no longer directly depends on mass_media.
would_reject_if: The consumerism node is later rescoped to broad mass culture rather than consumption and advertising-driven demand.
A stronger source shows non-advertising mass media was the direct prerequisite and advertising was only incidental.
A more precise postwar_television_advertising node is added and replaces advertising_mass_media_early.
short_reason: The direct media substrate is advertising, not generic mass media.
